t.name: t
t.description: The scope of submissions to search, such as `s` (complete submissions) or `all`.

qt.name: qt
qt.description: Default fields to search for query terms that do not name a field, such as `ti` or `au`.

reviewer.name: reviewer
reviewer.description: Email or ID of the reviewer whose viewpoint evaluates reviewer-relative search terms such as `myreview`.

sort.name: sort
sort.description: Sort order for the returned submissions, such as `id` or `-title`.

scoresort.name: scoresort
scoresort.description: Sort order for review-score fields, such as `average` or `counts`.

forceShow.name: forceShow
forceShow.description: Whether to override administrator conflicts. Defaults to `true`; set `forceShow=false` to respect conflicts instead.

final.summary: Document name for final version PDF
Document type for final version PDF

file.name: file
file.description: Filename of document uploaded to multi-document field

email.description: Email address. Most HotCRP email addresses are ordinary email addresses, but some are markers for anonymous or pseudonymous users (for example `anonymous`, `anonymous1`, …). These markers are accepted and returned wherever an email is expected, even though they are not valid email addresses.

role.description: A user’s standing in the conference. `chair` is a conference chair; `pc` is a program committee member (also present for chairs); `sysadmin` is a system administrator; `manager` administers some submissions without being a full chair; `author` is an author or contact of some submission; `reviewer` has been assigned at least one review.

potential_conflict.description: One program-committee member who potentially conflicts with a submission, as returned in the `potential_conflicts` array of [`/potentialconflicts`](#get-potentialconflicts).

uid.description: User ID of the PC member.

type.description: `author` when the PC member is an author of the submission; `potentialconflict` for a name, affiliation, or collaborator match.

description.description: Plain-text explanation of the match, as a formatted-text string beginning with the `<0>` format sigil. Present only when `type` is `potentialconflict`.

tooltip.description: HTML explanation of the match suitable for a tooltip, as a formatted-text string beginning with the `<5>` format sigil. Present only when `type` is `potentialconflict`.

sessioninfo.description: Information about the current session, including the CSRF token for cookie-authenticated requests. Returned by the `session` endpoints (and omitted from cross-origin responses).

postvalue.description: CSRF token. Supply this value in the `post` parameter to authorize cookie-authenticated `POST` requests.

email.description: Email of the signed-in user; omitted when no user is signed in

uid.description: User ID of the signed-in user; omitted when no user is signed in

job_status.description: Status of a long-running job. `wait` before the job starts, `run` while it is running, `done` after successful completion, and `failed` when it is known to have failed.

created_at.description: Time this version was first uploaded

attached_at.description: Most recent time this version was uploaded

active.description: True if this document is currently active

link.description: Link to this specific version of the document

hotlist.description: Opaque token that captures the search, including its description and the ordered list of submission IDs. Intended for HotCRP’s web client, which uses it to remember the list across page loads—most visibly to offer “previous”/“next” navigation between submissions and to support the tracker. External integrations should ignore this. Transmitted as a **JSON-encoded string** (a serialized form of the object documented here), not as a nested JSON object; HotCRP's client parses the string to recover these properties.

listid.description: Identifies the list and the search that generated it. Begins with the list type (`p` for submissions, `u` for users) and, for searches, encodes the query and its parameters, e.g. `p/s/1-40`.

description.description: Human-readable description of the list, e.g. `1-40 in Submitted`.

urlbase.description: Base URL of the page the list came from, used to reconstruct links back to it.

highlight.description: Whether items newly matching the list should be visually highlighted.

ids.description: The list members, in order, in HotCRP's compact run-length ID encoding. For long lists this may be omitted in favor of `digest` (see `hotlist_input`).

digest.description: Reference to a full list cached client-side (a token of the form `listdigest<N>`), sent in place of `ids` when the encoded list would be too long for a cookie.

curid.description: The submission currently being viewed from the list.

previd.description: The submission before `curid` in the list, for “previous” navigation.

nextid.description: The submission after `curid` in the list, for “next” navigation.

hotlist_input.description: A [`hotlist`](#tag-search) as accepted on input (for example in a `hotlist-info` parameter), extended with the navigation/caching fields HotCRP's client adds when storing a list: a `digest` reference for long lists, and the current/previous/next submission IDs.

encoded_pid_list.description: Encoded list of nonnegative submission IDs

encoded_search_parameters.description: URL-encoded string defining all relevant parameters for the search.

search_reviewer.description: Email of designated reviewer for search

search_parameter_specification.description: Encoded description of a search, as returned from the `search_params` response field of [`/search`](#get-search). Provide to recompute the search after the assignment. The response will include `ids`, `groups`, and `search_params` from that search.

search_response.opt.description: Search result whose properties are all optional. Use this where search results are merged into a response only under some conditions (for example, when a request supplies an optional `search` parameter); the same properties as `search_response`, but none are required.

search_response_ids.description: Array of submission IDs matching the search, in the order implied by the search.

search_response_groups.description: Array of [search annotations](#tag-search) corresponding to the search, sourced from tag annotations, headings, or `THEN` searches. Often empty.

conflict.description: True when this update was rejected by an if_unmodified_since edit-conflict check

comment_update_status.description: Status of a comment update operation

cid.description: The affected comment’s ID, when one was resolved

conflict.description: True when this update was rejected by an if_unmodified_since edit-conflict check

named_search.description: A named (saved) search. Returned by `/namedsearch`; PC members only.

display.description: Display options applied when the search is shown, as `show:`/`sort:` terms.

editable.description: Present and true when the caller may modify or delete the search.

named_formula.description: A named (saved) formula: a reusable name for a formula expression, usable in searches, scoring, and graphs. Returned by `/namedformula`.

editable.description: Present and true when the caller may modify or delete the formula.

message_list.description: Diagnostics produced when evaluating the expression; present only when the formula has problems.

setting_description.description: Machine-readable description of one setting—or, within `components`, one member field of an `object` or `oblist` setting. Returned by `/settingdescriptions`; names and types mirror the JSON that `/settings` accepts and returns. The text fields (`title`, `summary`, `description`) are formatted-text strings that begin with a format sigil, normally `<0>` for plain text.

name.description: Setting name: the key used for this setting in `/settings` JSON.

type.description: Value type of the setting, such as `string`, `int`, `checkbox`, `object`, or `oblist` (a list of objects).

description.description: Longer explanation of the setting (formatted text).

values.description: Example or permitted values for the setting.

default_value.description: The setting’s default value, in the same form `/settings` returns. Present only on top-level descriptors, not on `components` members.

components.description: For `object` and `oblist` settings, descriptors for the member fields, in order. Each member has the same shape as a top-level descriptor (without `default_value`).

tag_value_list.description: List of tag names with values

tag_decoration_html.description: HTML representation of badges and emoji

style_classes.description: Space-separated list of tag style classes

tag_annotation.description: Annotation dividing an ordered (ranking or votes) tag into labeled groups. Annotations are positioned by tag value: an annotation applies to the run of submissions whose value for the tag is at least its `tagval` (up to the next annotation).

annoid.description: Unique identifier for this annotation within the tag. (When creating an annotation via `POST /taganno`, supply a string beginning with `n`, such as `n1`, in place of an ID.)

tag.description: The tag this annotation belongs to.

tagval.description: Tag value at which the annotation begins; the annotation labels submissions whose value for the tag is at least this and less than the next annotation's value.

legend.description: Display text (heading) for the group. Absent for a blank separator.

format.description: Text format code for `legend`, present only when it is not the default format.

blank.description: Present and `true` when the annotation is a blank separator with no legend.

pos.description: Position of the annotation in the associated submission list, as an index into the result. Present only when the annotation is returned alongside a search result.

session_title.description: Optional session-scheduling metadata: the session's title, used when the tag organizes a meeting agenda.

time.description: Optional session-scheduling metadata: the session's time.

location.description: Optional session-scheduling metadata: the session's location.

session_chair.description: Optional session-scheduling metadata: the session's chair.

apiToken.description: External applications should authenticate to HotCRP’s API using bearer tokens. Users can create tokens via Account settings > Developer; tokens can have limited scope (e.g., read-only rights, or rights only for a subset of papers). API requests supply a token with the `Authorization` header: `Authorization: bearer TOKENNAME`.

session.name: hotcrpsession
session.description: Browser sessions authenticate to HotCRP’s API via a cookie. The name of the cookie varies by installation. Operations other than GET and HEAD also require a `post` query parameter for CSRF protection.
